evals.prompt: Write a monthly status report for March 2026. API uptime: 99.97%, Dashboard: 99.99%, Webhooks: 99.2%. Target is 99.9% for all. We had one major incident on March 14 (webhook failure, 47 min) and one minor (dashboard latency, 12 min). MTTR was 28 min, down from 45 min in Feb.
evals.expected_output: Should produce a complete monthly report with executive summary leading with headline number, uptime table with targets and month-over-month comparison, incident summaries with fixes, and improvement/looking-ahead sections.
assertions: Checks for .agents/status-page-context.md
Produces a monthly report using the template
Executive summary leads with headline uptime number
Uptime table includes SLA targets and vs-target comparison
Webhooks below-target is explicitly called out
Both incidents are summarized with severity, duration, and fix status
MTTR trend is shown (28 min vs 45 min)
Includes looking ahead section

evals.prompt: Write a quick weekly status report. No incidents this week. All systems above target. We have database maintenance scheduled next Tuesday.
evals.expected_output: Should produce a concise weekly report that states no incidents, confirms uptime, and mentions upcoming maintenance. Should be short (~1 page).
assertions: Produces a weekly report format
States no incidents clearly
Confirms systems are above target
Mentions upcoming maintenance with date
Keeps it concise (not a full monthly report)

evals.prompt: Write a Q1 2026 quarterly reliability report. Overall uptime: 99.94%. We had 2 critical incidents, 5 major, 8 minor. MTTR averaged 32 min. Error budget: 40% remaining. We shipped automated rollbacks and improved monitoring coverage from 60% to 85%.
evals.expected_output: Should produce a full quarterly report with multi-quarter trends (asking for previous quarter data if needed), SLA performance, top incidents, reliability investments, and outlook.
assertions: Produces a quarterly report format
Includes quarterly metrics table
Shows incident counts by severity
Includes MTTR and error budget status
Lists reliability investments shipped (automated rollbacks, monitoring)
Includes lessons learned section
Includes outlook for next quarter
Asks for previous quarter data for trends if not provided

evals.prompt: Write a monthly report. Our API was at 99.7% uptime this month, target is 99.9%. We had a 2-hour outage.
evals.expected_output: Should not hide or minimize the miss. Should explicitly call out the target gap, describe the incident, and connect it to fixes.
assertions: Explicitly states API missed its SLA target
Quantifies the gap (99.7% vs 99.9%)
Describes the outage that caused the miss
Asks about or mentions what was done to fix it
Does not use language that minimizes the miss

evals.prompt: Write a monthly status report. I don't really have exact uptime numbers.
evals.expected_output: Should ask what data is available, suggest how to get metrics, and offer to write a qualitative report if no numbers exist. Should not invent metrics.
assertions: Does not invent uptime numbers
Asks what data is available
Suggests where to get metrics (monitoring tools, status page history)
Offers a qualitative alternative if no numbers exist
Flags that quantitative reports are more valuable

evals.prompt: Write a monthly status report for our services
evals.expected_output: Should read .agents/status-page-context.md and use component names, SLA targets, and tone from context to structure the report.
assertions: Reads .agents/status-page-context.md
Uses component names from context
Uses SLA targets from context for comparison
Matches tone from context

evals.prompt: We're having an outage right now, can you write a report?
evals.expected_output: Should redirect to incident-communication for active incidents. Status reports are for periodic summaries, not live incidents.
assertions: Recognizes this is an active incident, not a periodic report
Points user to the incident-communication skill
Offers to help with the status report after the incident is resolved
